title: Postgres Connection Pool Exhaustion
description: All 100 Postgres connections were held open by a slow analytics query. The investigation engine identified the blocking query, retrieved a matching incident from semantic memory, and routed to manual approval for connection termination.

alert.detail: Postgres connection pool exhausted — 100/100 connections active, new connections refused

evidence_sources_used.detail: 18 errors — connection refused, pool wait timeout
2 historical matches — DB pool exhaustion Jan 2026, Feb 2026

hypotheses.cause: Long-running analytics query holding connections open
Connection leak in application ORM layer
Replica lag causing connection wait on primary

root_cause.summary: A scheduled analytics query (pid 8841, duration 847s) held 23 connections in 'idle in transaction' state. Combined with a connection leak in the API service (not closing connections on 5xx paths), the pool reached capacity. Historical memory confirmed identical pattern from Feb 2026 — same query, same fix.
supporting_evidence: Long query pid 8841: SELECT ... FROM events JOIN ... (847s elapsed)
API service logs: ConnectionPoolTimeoutError x18 in 5 minutes
Historical match Feb 2026: same query pattern, resolved by pg_terminate_backend
pg_locks: 14 lock waits on relation 'events', holder pid 8841
reasoning_steps: CPU 38% with pool exhaustion → not a compute problem, likely lock/connection contention
pg_connections shows 23 idle-in-transaction entries >600s each — abnormal hold
pg_long_queries confirms pid 8841 at 847s — analytics job that started outside business hours
Semantic memory match: Feb 2026 same pattern, terminated pid, pool recovered in 30s
Connection leak compounds: API not closing on 5xx means even after kill, pool stays near limit

planner_steps.reason: connection exhaustion often caused by long transactions
confirm whether long query is causing lock cascades

memory_matches.title: DB Pool Exhaustion — Feb 2026
memory_matches.root_cause: Analytics query held idle-in-transaction connections, pg_terminate_backend resolved

memory_matches.title: DB Pool Exhaustion — Jan 2026
memory_matches.root_cause: ORM connection leak on 5xx paths, patched in v1.4.2

outcome.resolution_note: Connection pool recovered to 12/100 within 45 seconds. Fix confirmed: added connection.close() to 5xx handler in API service.
